qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in /api/download allows arbitrary file read via flawed ../ sanitization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable endpoint and root cause: this finding concerns REST GET /api/download filename sanitization and default-disabled authentication, while the advisory concerns unauthenticated Socket.IO device tag writes and missing write authorization checks even when secure mode is enabled.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via /api/runscript test mode
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable endpoint and root cause: this finding concerns REST POST /api/runscript compiling attacker-supplied JavaScript through Module._compile with default-disabled authentication, while the advisory concerns unauthenticated Socket.IO device tag writes and missing write authorization checks even when secure mode is enabled.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. GHSA-ggxw-g3cp-mgf8 describes an authorization bypass in FUXA Socket.IO/WebSocket device tag writes, allowing unauthenticated remote modification of device tags and disabling drivers. The referenced patch adds Socket.IO write authorization checks in server/runtime/index.js for device value set and device enable operations. The qualifying findings instead cover unrelated REST endpoint issues in /api/runscript and /api/download.
evidence: Advisory summary/details: unauthenticated remote attacker can modify device tags via WebSockets and bypass role-based access controls, including with runtime.settings.secureEnabled set to true.
Referenced patch eb2d8a20964ce7acaa0f442a181390a5f726a1ae adds isSocketWriteAuthorized(socket) and guards Socket.IO DEVICE_VALUES set and DEVICE_ENABLE writes in server/runtime/index.js.
rce-runscript/vulnerability_report.json describes POST /api/runscript JavaScript compilation through Module._compile and default-disabled authentication, not Socket.IO device tag write authorization.

path-traversal-download/vulnerability_report.json describes GET /api/download path traversal through flawed ../ sanitization and default-disabled authentication, not Socket.IO device tag write authorization.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matched the advisory-described WebSocket device tag write authorization bypass.
